title: Using nftables on a Windows WSL machine (amd64) doesn't work
body:
### Issue Description

After updating to Fedora 41 and configuring the machine in rootful mode, running a container returns a netavark error:

```log
# podman run hello
WARN[0000] Using cgroups-v1 which is deprecated in favor of cgroups-v2 with Podman v5 and will be removed in a future version. Set environment variable `PODMAN_IGNORE_CGROUPSV1_WARNING` to hide this warning.
internal:0:0-0: Error: Could not process rule: No such file or directory

internal:0:0-0: Error: Could not process rule: No such file or directory

Error: netavark: nftables error: nft did not return successfully while applying ruleset
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Init/Run a rootful machine: 
```
> cd <podman git repo>
> gh pr checkout 26318
> .\winmake.ps1 podman
> .\bin\windows\podman machine init --rootful --now
> .\bin\windows\podman podman run hello
```

### podman info output

```yaml
Podman built from main branch running on Windows 11 with WSL v2.4.9.0 and Kernel v5.15.167.4-1
```

### Additional information

Using a more recent version of the Kernel (v6.6.36) doesn't help.

Configuring podman to use iptables fixes the problem: 
```
[network]
firewall_driver="iptables"
```

comments.body:
Tested a rootful WSL machine with a simple nftables ruleset and got no error:

```
# cat << EOF > /tmp/simplerule
table inet filter {
       chain input {
           type filter hook input priority 0;
           policy drop;

           # Allow established/related connections
           ct state established,related accept

           # Allow HTTP
           tcp dport 80 accept

           # Allow HTTPS
           tcp dport 443 accept
       }
   }
EOF
# nft -f /tmp/simplerule
# nft list ruleset
table inet filter {
        chain input {
                type filter hook input priority filter; policy drop;
                ct state established,related accept
                tcp dport 80 accept
                tcp dport 443 accept
        }
}
```

However netawark tests in [250-bridge-nftables.bats](https://github.com/containers/netavark/blob/main/test/250-bridge-nftables.bats) failed:

```
# dnf install -y make rust cargo protobuf-compiler bats jq
# cd /mnt/c/Users/<folder where netavark was cloned>
# make
# bats ./test/250-bridge-nftables.bats -f "check nftables driver is in use"
250-bridge-nftables.bats
 ✗ check nftables driver is in use
   (from function `die' in file test/helpers.bash, line 249,
    from function `run_helper' in file test/helpers.bash, line 233,
    from function `run_in_host_netns' in file test/helpers.bash, line 166,
    from function `run_netavark' in file test/helpers.bash, line 136,
    in test file test/250-bridge-nftables.bats, line 12)
     `RUST_LOG=netavark=info run_netavark --file ${TESTSDIR}/testfiles/simplebridge.json setup $(get_container_netns_path)' failed
    nsenter -n -m -w -t 14493 ip link set lo up
    nsenter -n -m -w -t 14493 ./bin/netavark --rootless false --config /tmp/netavark_bats.1qVMcE/config --file /mnt/c/Users/mario/Git/netavark/test/testfiles/simplebridge.json setup /proc/14495/ns/net
   [INFO  netavark::firewall] Using nftables firewall driver
   [INFO  netavark::network::netlink] Adding route (dest: 0.0.0.0/0 ,gw: 10.88.0.1, metric 100)
   [INFO  netavark::firewall::nft] Creating container chain nv_53ce4390_10_88_0_0_nm16
   internal:0:0-0: Error: Could not process rule: No such file or directory

   internal:0:0-0: Error: Could not process rule: No such file or directory

   {"error":"nftables error: nft did not return successfully while applying ruleset"}
   [ rc=1 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #| FAIL: exit code is 1; expected 0
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

1 test, 1 failure
```
@Luap99 any idea why running `nft` manually works but netawark fails? Is there a `nft` ruleset I can test?

Hello @l0rd 
I am facing the same issue when executing `podman-compose --in-pod=1 --project-name myproject -f ./docker-compose.yml up -d`, Have you found a solution for that yet?

You should not be facing the problem if you are using the default Podman machine. The problem is with nftables and the WSLv2 kernel but we are currently configuring the WSL machine to use iptables rather than the nftables.

> You should not be facing the problem if you are using the default Podman machine. The problem is with nftables and the WSLv2 kernel but we are currently configuring the WSL machine to use iptables rather than the nftables.

Yeah, I am using WSL2 with the Fedora 42 beta WSL image and facing the issue. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3ad7574a-0303-4d1b-9cc1-24afbcfcce40)

You can still manually configure podman to use iptables by adding the following lines to the `/etc/containers/containers.conf` (that's what [we currently do automatically when creating the WSL machine](https://github.com/l0rd/podman/blob/dae1541529cc2810fd603460f55f12291ac6ccbb/pkg/machine/wsl/declares.go#L11)):

```
[network]
firewall_driver="iptables"
```

> You can still manually configure podman to use iptables by adding the following lines to the `/etc/containers/containers.conf` (that's what [we currently do automatically when creating the WSL machine](https://github.com/l0rd/podman/blob/dae1541529cc2810fd603460f55f12291ac6ccbb/pkg/machine/wsl/declares.go#L11)):
> 
> ```
> [network]
> firewall_driver="iptables"
> ```

Thank you, it worked now

Just because it may help somehow.

I get also with the docker deskop and the wsl integration a similar error besides podman inside the wsl.

The switch to iptables worked for me with podman.

> firewall_driver="iptables"

For others coming across this, you also need** to install either `iptables-nft` or `iptables-legacy`

 ** on the OOTB WSL2 Fedora, at least. CF: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16956#issuecomment-1368502060

Could you try this on the latest version of WSL: 2.5.7 ?

@craigloewen-msft Note to force the use of nftables you must either have a rootful machine or use
```
podman run --network bridge hello
```

In any case we will test again. Though I don't see a 2.5.9 release here https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/releases?

With WSL-2.5.7 this seems to work now and I believe we can close this.  I have verified with my windows box here. @l0rd given that you reported this, can you `wsl --update` and see if it now works for you?
@Luap99 I meant to type in `2.5.7` but typed `2.5.9` instead!
That's great! Unfortunately I am traveling and I don't have the Windows laptop with me. I will check in a couple of days.

I am not able to reproduce the issue anymore. Thank you @craigloewen-msft for letting us know. I am closing this issue as completed. 

I have opened https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26318 to revert the WSL specific config to use iptables.
We should revert https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-wsl-os/commit/cb199ac2e7a64013ecf3b46b77e567978290a8b5 too but considereda that @Luap99 is moving the WSL image to `containers/podman-machine-os` we should fix that there instead.

I'm currently using WSL 2.5.7 on Windows 10.0.19045 Build 19045 and the problem still exists.
I removed iptables like in the PR https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26318 from the 5.5 and 5.6 versions of the quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl images and I still get the following errors:

```
internal:0:0-0: Error: Could not process rule: No such file or directory

internal:0:0-0: Error: Could not process rule: No such file or directory

Error: netavark: nftables error: "nft" did not return successfully while applying ruleset
```
The official FedoraLinux-42 WSL image is also impacted by the same error.

> > You can still manually configure podman to use iptables by adding the following lines to the `/etc/containers/containers.conf` (that's what [we currently do automatically when creating the WSL machine](https://github.com/l0rd/podman/blob/dae1541529cc2810fd603460f55f12291ac6ccbb/pkg/machine/wsl/declares.go#L11)):
> > ```
> > [network]
> > firewall_driver="iptables"
> > ```
> 
> Thank you, it worked now

This workaround works for the three images currently.

@J-PC can you please provide detailed instructions on how to reproduce your problem? 

I am testing the command `podman run hello`:
- using the podman binary from PR #26318 
- with podman configured as rootful
- with the default image `quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl:5.6`

The command fails with WSL v2.4.13.0 and succeeds with v2.5.7.0.

And when the command failed, I got a different message compared to what you shared:
```
Error: preparing container 852c8a664e89c58c68aa8dcd42d0e29654a79b13ebeb1ddb176a382a6f3e8ed7 for attach: netavark (exit code 1): nftables error: "nft" did not return successfully while applying ruleset` 
```

I have tested the same scenario on an x64 laptop and I could reproduce the problem. Reopening this issue:

@craigloewen-msft no, those instructions were valid before we merged #25202. Currently, to reproduce the issue, you need to first checkout PR #26318 and build podman. I ~~will update~~ have updated the instructions in the description because it's confusing:

```pwsh
cd <podman git repo>
gh pr checkout 26318
.\winmake.ps1 podman
.\bin\windows\podman machine init --rootful --now
.\bin\windows\podman run hello
```

Do you have a link to the kernel config used by WSL? I am not sure why it differs but AFAIK wsl doesn't use the dynamically loaded modules and nft complains about the NFT_FIB module missing from the error above. 

That is what I have on my fedora system.
```
$ grep FIB  /boot/config-6.14.9-200.fc41.x86_64
CONFIG_IP_FIB_TRIE_STATS=y
CONFIG_NFT_FIB=m
CONFIG_NFT_FIB_INET=m
CONFIG_NFT_FIB_NETDEV=m
CONFIG_NFT_FIB_IPV4=m
CONFIG_NFT_FIB_IPV6=m
CONFIG_FIB_RULES=y

```

For the x86 `CONFIG_NFT_FIB_INET=m` and `CONFIG_NFT_FIB_NETDEV=m` aren't configured.

```bash
$ # ARM64
$ curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/microsoft/WSL2-Linux-Kernel/refs/heads/linux-msft-wsl-6.6.y/arch/arm64/configs/config-wsl-arm64 | grep FIB
CONFIG_IP_FIB_TRIE_STATS=y
CONFIG_NFT_FIB=m
CONFIG_NFT_FIB_INET=m
CONFIG_NFT_FIB_NETDEV=m
CONFIG_NFT_FIB_IPV4=m
CONFIG_NFT_FIB_IPV6=m
CONFIG_FIB_RULES=y
$
$ # x86
$ curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/microsoft/WSL2-Linux-Kernel/refs/heads/linux-msft-wsl-6.6.y/arch/x86/configs/config-wsl | grep FIB
CONFIG_IP_FIB_TRIE_STATS=y
CONFIG_NFT_FIB=m
CONFIG_NFT_FIB_IPV4=m
# CONFIG_NFT_FIB_IPV6 is not set
CONFIG_FIB_RULES=y
```

m means it is compiled as module, AFAIK nftables will load it automatically. But how is module loading even supposed to work on WSL2? At least with regular linux distros the modules are provided under /usr where modprobe would find and load them. But I don't think that is a thing with WSL2 where everything must be compiled in?

> But how is module loading even supposed to work on WSL2?

You need to provide modules in a vhdx, as described in https://github.com/microsoft/WSL2-Linux-Kernel?tab=readme-ov-file#build-instructions (and indeed, you can fix the issue by building your own kernel including the missing modules)

I requested the missing kernel modules in https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13479, maybe someone from this issue can prod the right people at Microsoft? 🥺
Note we have strong plans to drop the iptables driver with podman 6 next year so this really should be fixed on WSL before then so it can work correctly with nftables.

Latest version of WSL still doesn't work on x86 (`v2.6.1.0` with kernel ` 6.6.87.2-1`).

WSL Kernel recompiled with the config suggested [here](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13479) by @TobiX works fine. ~~doesn't seem to work neither. But the error is different, compared to the default Kernel, when running `podman run hello`~~:

```diff
-- Error: preparing container <...> for attach: netavark (exit code 1): nftables error: "nft" did not return successfully while applying ruleset
++ Error: preparing container <...> for attach: netavark (exit code 1): create bridge: Netlink error: Operation not supported (os error 95)
```

~~@Luap99 any idea of other modules that may be missing?~~

**EDIT** After a second try I was able to use the Kernel config suggested by @TobiX to maek Podman work fine with nftables on WSL.
And `CONFIG_VETH` as well so it can create the veth pair

I gave it another try, and with the Kernel rebuilt with the config changes in https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13479, rootful podman works with nftables. (When I tried yesterday, I wasn't loading any module, which explained why it was failing earlier.) So, if https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13479 gets fixed, this problem will also be fixed. cc @craigloewen-msft

I believe https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13479 is ultimately a duplicate of https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/6044 which is open for a long time. I really hope the default wsl kernel can enable the missing nftables modules soon so we can fully transition of iptables

> I believe https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13479 is ultimately a duplicate of https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/6044 which is open for a long time. I really hope the default wsl kernel can enable the missing nftables modules soon so we can fully transition of iptables

Itseems to only address the x86 hope aarch64 would also be taken care of!!!
